RQ3 (intersectional): Cochran's Q for homogeneous gender odds ratios across backgrounds: Q=0.7, p=0.9507. Logistic interaction LR test (gender × background, controlling income+apartment): χ²=2.58, df=4, p=0.6307. No significant gender×background interaction after controlling income.
BackgroundMaleFemaleGap (F−M)
Local58/240 = 24.2%59/238 = 24.8%+0.6pp
EU60/240 = 25.0%64/238 = 26.9%+1.9pp
Non-EU65/240 = 27.1%73/240 = 30.4%+3.3pp
2nd gen60/238 = 25.2%69/240 = 28.8%+3.6pp
Refugee98/238 = 41.2%98/240 = 40.8%-0.4pp

Income ablation (remove income confound)

Within each income stratum, employment/marital/children still vary across sets — use medium-income slice or set fixed-effects for cleanest ablation.

Income stratumnGender χ² (p)Background χ² (p)Gender gap
Low8000.0, p=1.020.68, p=0.0004+0.2pp
Medium8000.14, p=0.70718.91, p=0.0008+1.6pp
High7921.0, p=0.317926.14, p=0.0+3.8pp
Medium-income ablation (n=800): background χ² p=0.0008; Cochran Q p=0.9356. Refugee advantage is strongest at medium income (46.9% Yes).
